pages.raw_text:
Northern Pacific railroad officials
will file with the city council on
Nov. l plans for grade separation
of their line through the southern
part of the city. This was decided
upon at the st. Paul headquarters
the other day, when the plans were
gone over and adopted.


These plans are now in the city,
but have not yet been turned over
to city officials or anyone interest
ed by railroad people.


1t means that the fight for grade
separation which has been waged
by city officials unremittingly for
many years has at last been won.


In past years numerous people
have been killed and injured on
the unguarded crossing, particular
ly at Division st and Sprague av..
where for almost the entire day and
a portion Of the nights trains are
switched back and forth in the
course of being made up to send
out east and west. AT other cross
ings, delapidated gates are some
times used, but very infrequently,
and pedestrians are compelled to
take their chances ON luck and
agility. The practice of leaving
cars on crossings to block traffic
ration the city has kept up a sickly
sort Of controversy trying to have
the company put in proper and
safe up-to-date gates for the public's
protection. The company has
steadfastly refused to comply and
has even refused TO station flagmen
at those crossings. And complaisant
city officials went no farthur than
feeble protestation, naturally.


The grade separation will begin
east OF the shops east OF Division
